key_metrics: Billed business of $387.3 billion, up 9% YoY
Total loans and Card Member receivables of $216.4 billion, up 7% YoY
Net interest yield of 8.2%, up from 8.0% YoY
qoq_changes: Net card fee revenue growth moderated from the prior quarter due to the previous cycle of product refreshes
Launched refreshed U.S. Consumer and Business Platinum Cards with expanded benefits and increased annual fees
new_risks: Uncertainty of the geopolitical and macroeconomic environment
Evolving competitive landscape and changes in response to evolving technologies and business dynamics
management_tone: Bullish, driven by record levels of Card Member spending, excellent credit performance, and confidence in their Membership-focused business model and product refresh strategy.
analyst_note: American Express delivered a strong Q3 2025 with 11% revenue growth and 16% net income growth, supported by robust Millennial and Gen-Z spending, stable credit metrics, and successful premium card refreshes.
